# Gillnet 

After the common components of the gillnet form, there are three sections for gear data each with an observed trip UI. The first identifies the presence or absence of a net drum hauler. The second section is for configuration of gillnets data, specifically panel characteristics, stretched mesh sizes, the colour of the net, and float and dropline data. The last gear section is for sinker configuration data. The gillnet configuration data and sinker configuration data are linked by the gillnet sequential number data field.  

The next two sections of the form are for setting operation data, and include both trip ID and Set ID UIs. In addition to start and end of setting data, there is also setting strategy and mitigation measure data. The second section is for hauling operations. This section also has start and end data, but for hauling operations, as well as net condition data.  

Following setting and hauling is catch data. The first section summarized catch for the whole fishing trip. Each row has an assigned trip ID, set ID, and catch ID. Then fates, numbers of fish, and weights are reported for each species. Specimen and SSI data sections start with the addition of a specimen ID UI. Catch data for each specimen includes conditions of non-target species. For all species, depredation details, length, weight, sex, and maturity data are collected. Additional fields cover sample collection and tag details. The SSI section maintain the same resolution and has data fields for gear interactions, if the species was brought onboard, and how it was handled.
